properties.Other names: El Colorín, Colorado de la Mora, Salvador Allende (two Wixarika towns along periphery)
properties.Ecological/Environmental: One of several dams built along Santiago River since the 1980s. El Cajón Dam is just a few kilometers downriver. Located in a key area ecologically where there are vasts amounts of fresh water, mountains with biodiversity, and by...
properties.Socio-Political: The dam displaced several Wixarika communities (many of these individuals would become residents of Colonia Zitakua in Tepic) and was built during the controversial Salinas de Gortari presidency

properties.Ecological/Environmental: A rare volcanic remnant in an area otherwise without volcanic arqueology. Was at one point ocean, there are shell remnants in the sierra of catorce
properties.Cultural Elements: Important site of pilgrimage for the community of Tateikié and other Wixárika communities; a popular destination for new agers

properties.Ecological/Environmental: traverses central mexico up and through the US-Mx border
Sierra of Catorce, site of colonial mining, forests continue to struggle to grow
properties.Cultural Elements: Site where the sun was born after a boy sacrificed self
properties.Socio-Political: Site of continued contested land use and First Majestic Silver mining project under moratorium

properties.Ecological/Environmental: Located near the endemic areas where peyote grows
properties.Cultural Elements: Site of New Age tourism and Willies that take tourists to extract peyote
properties.Socio-Political: Many of the land privatizations occurring in the name of peyote conservation are springing from negotiations with ejidatarios in this town and municipality

properties.Ecological/Environmental: Located in Lake Chapala, itself under high environmental alert due to the convergence of contaminants from the Lerma and Santiago rivers
properties.Cultural Elements: A sacred place for the Wixarika and Coca communities; recently also a location of New Age ritual
properties.Socio-Political: Location of a colonial presidio that kept its prisoners in horrific conditions. The presidio was seized by independence insurgents in an epic battle that marks the beginning of Mexico's war of independence

properties.Places and Items: Island of the Scorpion/Isla de los Alacranes
properties.Ecological/Environmental: Located in Lake Chapala, itself under high environmental alert due to the convergence of contaminants from the Lerma and Santiago rivers. The island has long had thin environmental and cultural regulations but is now being brought into the management plans drafted as part of the Plan de Justicia Wixárika
properties.Cultural Elements: Since the late 20th century, the principal destination of Wixarika pilgrimages to Xapawiyemetá
properties.Socio-Political: Wixarika offerings are mixed with an increased presence of non-Indigenous
offerings. The xiriki (temple) has repeatedly burned and in May 2024, an obelisk was placed by the government to make more visible the place as a Wixarika sacred site

properties.Ecological/Environmental: Site of some tourist development conflicts, very important ecosystem--also famously known for the
properties.Cultural Elements: Pilgrimage site of the west for all communities, related to our Grandmother Ocean. Important for baptisms, related to the underworld and darkness, source of life
properties.Socio-Political: Small tourist hamlet with ocassional disputes over encroachment onto Waxiewe, the white island off the shore of San Blas. Recently declared a protected sacred site in Mexico's

properties.Ecological/Environmental: In the canyons of the Sierra madre
properties.Cultural Elements: A sacred site for all communities
properties.Socio-Political: Contentious as of lately because of intercommunal conflict

properties.Ecological/Environmental: Town founded in the early 19th century from colonial silver mining boom, today a site of religious tourism
properties.Cultural Elements: San Francisco de Asis/ Wixarika pilgrimages
